title: Week 1 Retention Tracking
content:
Week 1 Retention Tracking

Hello, x! I see that you have not yet logged onto Blackboard and accessed the course. I wanted to let you know that this is a four-week course and there's a lot of work to be done in a brief amount of time. Let's set up a time to chat if you need further assistance.

Best,
Professor Padavano

Student failed to login to Blackboard and access the course. I reached out to the student via their Keiser email address and their secondary email address to arrange for a meeting to discuss the class.

—————————

Week 1 Discussion Forum

Thank you for your post, x!

In future postings, avoid submitting blocks of text and instead consider breaking your ideas into smaller, more digestible paragraphs. By doing so, you will begin to focus on paying closer attention to how one idea flows into the next, creating smoother transitions.

The discussion posts require you to respond to at least two peer's posts in a substantial and meaningful manner (~150 words). The goal here is to further along the conversation and achieve a higher learning. Challenge each other's thoughts, ask questions, or perhaps agree but with a difference. Promote further discussion on the subject matter through thought-provoking peer responses; demonstrate depth of analysis of topic and peer’s post; reveal evident reliance on the primary sources.
context: From document: Week 1 Retention Tracking — chunk 1 (chunk 1/133)
